description_long: This is a laboratory-style Erlenmeyer flask with a wide, conical glass body that tapers smoothly to a narrow cylindrical neck. The flask is partially filled with a greenish liquid, which is visible through the transparent glass walls. The neck of the bottle is topped with a brownish cap or stopper, which appears to be made of a different material than the body. The glass surface is smooth and glossy, reflecting light subtly. The overall form is symmetrical and stable, designed to rest securely on flat surfaces. There are no visible labels, markings, or branding on the exterior.
description: A transparent Erlenmeyer flask with a green liquid inside and a brown cap, featuring a smooth, glossy glass surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing the conical glass body, green liquid, and brown cap clearly.
description_view_1: Side view nearly identical to the front, with the same visibility of the liquid and cap.
description_view_2: Rear view, similar to the front, with no additional features or markings visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the conical shape, liquid, and cap without distinguishing marks.
